team_context.cap_situation: tight — ~$8-9M effective cap space after Watson restructure, Deshaun Watson's dead money dominates the cap through 2027. Restructured Ward's deal for modest savings. Approximately $30M opened via Watson restructure but heavy spending in free agency on OL (Howard, Zion Johnson, Elgton Jenkins) and LB (Quincy Williams) has consumed most space.
team_context.trade_posture: Strategic pivot toward youth. New HC Todd Monken installing new offense. Team went 5-12 in 2025 with the 31st-ranked scoring offense. Elite defense anchored by Myles Garrett (23 sacks, DPOY). Willing to move veterans who don't fit the rebuild timeline, but core young pieces (2025 draft class) are untouchable. Two first-round picks (#6, #24) in 2026 draft provide flexibility.

players.name: Shedeur Sanders
players.performance_evidence: Completed 56.6% of passes for 1,400 yards, 7 TD, 10 INT, 68.1 passer rating in 8 games (7 starts). Last among qualifying QBs in completion%, passer rating, and adjusted yards per attempt. However, showed flashes — 364 yards and 4 total TDs vs Titans in Week 14, joined Burrow as only rookies with 350+ passing yards, 3 pass TDs and a rush TD in same game. Named Pro Bowl replacement. Played behind the worst OL in football (23 sacks in 8 games). Very raw but high ceiling given draft pedigree and arm talent.

trade_value.reasoning: Young QB on a dirt-cheap 5th-round rookie deal with 3 years of control. Extremely raw stats but showed enough to be the presumptive starter. The combination of low cost and positional value gives him more trade value than his on-field production suggests, but his poor efficiency and turnover issues cap the ceiling. Worth a late 3rd/early 4th to a QB-needy team.

players.availability_reasoning: Browns haven't committed to Sanders as franchise QB — GM Berry said both Sanders and Watson are 'in the mix' for QB1. If Cleveland drafts a QB or adds a veteran, Sanders could become available. But he's the cheapest possible starting QB option, so team has no incentive to sell low.
players.character_notes: High-profile name (son of Deion Sanders) generates media attention. Had sideline confrontation with Jerry Jeudy during 2025 season.
players.trade_buzz: Beat reporters note the Browns are looking into free agent QBs and haven't ruled out drafting one. Sanders' job security is uncertain under new HC Todd Monken.

players.name: Dillon Gabriel
players.performance_evidence: Started 5 games in 2025: 58.6% completion, 869 yards, 7 TD, 2 INT. Ranked 32nd of 33 qualifying QBs in QBR (32.5), last in yards per attempt (5.0). Six-year college player brought in for scheme versatility but was unable to elevate the offense.

trade_value.reasoning: Cheap backup QB on a rookie deal, but showed very little in his 5 starts. Multiple outlets predict the Browns will trade him for a 5th or 6th rounder. Minimal standalone value.

players.availability_reasoning: 247Sports projects Browns will 'talk Gabriel up and then trade him away at some point this offseason for a 5th or 6th rounder.' With Sanders and Watson on the roster, Gabriel is redundant.
players.trade_buzz: Beat reporters expect Gabriel to be traded this offseason. Third QB on the depth chart with no path to starting.

players.name: Quinshon Judkins
players.performance_evidence: 827 rushing yards, 7 TDs on 230 carries (3.6 YPC) plus 26 receptions for 171 yards in 14 games as a rookie. Led all rookie RBs in rushing yards. The 3.6 YPC is misleading — faced stacked boxes 45.2% of the time (highest in NFL) due to Browns' terrible passing game. Pro Bowl alternate. Showed power, vision, and receiving ability.

trade_value.reasoning: Young starting RB on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control. RB position value is low, but Judkins showed legitimate starter-quality play despite awful supporting cast. Worth a mid-3rd rounder. Ankle injury is a concern but expected full recovery.

players.availability_reasoning: Cornerstone of the rebuild. Young, cheap, and the Browns' best offensive player in 2025. No chance they move him after one season.
players.injury_notes: Dislocated right ankle and fractured fibula in Week 16 of 2025. Underwent surgery. GM Berry said at Combine he has shed his walking boot and is on track for training camp.
players.character_notes: Misdemeanor domestic violence charge in July 2025 that South Florida prosecutors declined to formally pursue. Delayed his contract signing and integration into the team.

players.name: Dylan Sampson
players.performance_evidence: Change-of-pace back as a rookie. Displayed receiving ability — had a 66-yard screen pass TD in Shedeur Sanders' first start. Speed and agility are his calling card. Limited sample size in a bad offense.

trade_value.reasoning: Cheap young backup RB with some receiving upside. 4th-round rookie deal. Not enough production yet to command meaningful trade value, but has development potential.

players.availability_reasoning: Young, cheap depth piece. Browns have no incentive to move him unless blown away by an offer.
players.injury_notes: Dealt with hand and calf injuries late in 2025 season.

players.name: Jerry Jeudy
players.performance_evidence: Had a Pro Bowl season in 2024 (90 catches, 1,229 yards) but regressed sharply in 2025: approximately 50 catches, 650 yards in a full season. PFF grade crashed from 73.5 to 56.9 (106th of 130 WRs). 9 drops in 2025 after 10 in 2024. Sideline confrontation with Shedeur Sanders. Some of the decline is attributable to the Browns' historically bad QB play and OL.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 1st-round WR still just 27, showed he can produce (2024 Pro Bowl). But 2025 was terrible, contract has $6M guaranteed option bonus that must be exercised by the 15th day of the 2026 league year, and dead cap ($16.7M) exceeds cap hit ($10.4M) making a trade a cap loss. Worth a late 3rd/4th to a team betting on the 2024 version, but the dead cap makes moving him painful.

players.availability_reasoning: Spotrac's Mike Ginnitti identified Jeudy as a roster 'bubble' candidate. The $6M 2026 option bonus is already guaranteed, but Browns can trade him before it must be exercised (March 25). Dead cap exceeds cap hit by ~$6.3M, so there's a cap loss on trade. But if he doesn't fit Todd Monken's offense, Cleveland may take the hit for draft compensation.
players.character_notes: Sideline confrontation with QB Shedeur Sanders during 2025 season. Effort questions emerged during poor 2025 campaign.
players.trade_buzz: Spotrac labeled him a 'bubble' candidate. Beat reporters suggest he may not return for the next iteration of the Browns offense. However, dead cap complications make an outright trade financially painful.

players.name: Cedric Tillman
players.performance_evidence: 21 catches for 224 yards as a 2023 rookie, 29 catches for 339 yards and 3 TDs in 2024, then just 21 catches for ~250 yards in 2025 — missed significant time with a hamstring injury (placed on IR for 4 weeks). Has shown flashes (2 TDs in first 2 games of 2025) but hasn't established himself as a reliable WR2.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 3rd-round pick on a final-year cheap deal. Has some talent but hasn't put it together consistently. Expiring contract limits value. Worth a late-round pick at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Final year of his rookie deal. If Browns draft or sign WR help, Tillman becomes expendable. Cheap enough to keep as depth but not integral to the rebuild.
players.injury_notes: Hamstring injury cost him 4 weeks on IR in 2025. Also dealt with concussion/rib issues later in season.

players.name: Isaiah Bond
players.performance_evidence: Showed big-play ability as a rookie — connected with Shedeur Sanders on multiple 40+ yard plays. Led the team with approximately 338 receiving yards as a deep threat. Speed is his calling card. Very raw route runner but has developmental upside.

trade_value.reasoning: 5th-round rookie WR on a minimum deal with speed and big-play ability. Too early in development to command meaningful trade value, but the combination of youth, cost, and speed has some appeal.

players.availability_reasoning: Cheap developmental piece. Browns have no reason to move him unless they receive a surprisingly good offer.
players.injury_notes: Missed time with a foot injury during 2025 season.

players.name: Harold Fannin Jr.
players.performance_evidence: Led the Browns in receptions (72), receiving yards (731), and TDs (6) as a rookie TE — exceptional production for a 3rd-round pick. Pro Bowl alternate. Set the Browns' rookie receptions record. Only the second rookie TE in Browns history with a 100-yard game (joining HOFer Ozzie Newsome). Coordinator Tommy Rees called him a player who 'doesn't play like a rookie.' Showed strong YAC ability and route running.

trade_value.reasoning: 21-year-old starting TE who led the team in receiving as a rookie, on a cheap 3rd-round deal with 3 years of control. This is an emerging star at an increasingly valued position. With David Njoku departing, Fannin is THE TE1. Worth a mid-to-late 2nd round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: The Browns' best offensive player going forward. 21 years old, ultra-cheap, led the team in all receiving categories as a rookie. Njoku just left — Fannin IS the TE position. Zero chance he's moved.
players.injury_notes: Reinjured groin in Week 17 win over Steelers, missed Week 18 finale. Expected to be fully healthy for 2026.

players.name: Blake Whiteheart
players.performance_evidence: Blocking-oriented TE2. Very limited receiving production. Depth piece behind Fannin with newly signed Jack Stoll also in the mix.

trade_value.reasoning: Minimum-salary TE depth. No meaningful trade value.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth piece. Would be moved for any reasonable offer.

players.name: Dawand Jones
players.performance_evidence: Showed promise as a rookie in 2023 (PFWA All-Rookie team) but has been injury-plagued. Season-ending knee injury in Week 3 of 2025 (torn LCL, hamstring off bone). Also had ankle surgery ending 2024 season. When healthy, he's a massive (6-8, 374 lbs) tackle with upside — didn't allow a sack as a college starter. But availability is a major concern.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, cheap OT with starter upside on a final-year deal, but severe injury history (season-ending injuries in 2024 AND 2025) tanks his value. Worth a 5th-round pick to a team willing to take the injury gamble.

players.availability_reasoning: Final year of his rookie contract. Browns signed Tytus Howard and may draft an OT at #6 or #24. Jones is the only returning 2025 OL starter but his injury history makes him unreliable. If healthy, he's a valuable swing tackle. If not, he's expendable.
players.injury_notes: Torn LCL with hamstring off the bone in Week 3 of 2025. Also had season-ending ankle surgery in 2024. GM Berry says rehab is 'right on track' and he could be ready for OTAs. Major durability red flag.

players.name: Zion Johnson
players.performance_evidence: Former 17th overall pick who played 100% of Chargers offensive snaps in 2025. Struggled as a pass blocker (5th-lowest PBWR among guards) but registered the 2nd-best run block win rate. Versatile — played RG as a rookie before switching to LG in 2023. The Chargers let him walk, which says something about his inconsistency.

trade_value.reasoning: 26-year-old former 1st-round guard on a new 3-year deal with heavy guarantees ($32.4M). The guaranteed money makes him nearly impossible to trade — dead cap would be massive. He's a below-average pass protector but strong run blocker, locked in for 3 years.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed to a 3-year, $49.5M deal with $32.4M guaranteed. The dead cap makes him untradeable. Browns need him as a cornerstone of the OL rebuild.

players.name: Elgton Jenkins
players.performance_evidence: 2x Pro Bowler (2020, 2022) with elite versatility — 4,161 snaps at LG, 906 at C, 524 at LT, 374 at RT in Green Bay. Moved to full-time center in 2025 and ranked 7th in pass blocking among centers per PFF before suffering a fractured fibula in Week 9. Released by Packers with a failed physical designation. Still a viable starter when healthy but run blocking has declined.

trade_value.reasoning: Veteran center with Pro Bowl pedigree on a 2-year deal, but $20M guaranteed makes him nearly untradeable given dead cap implications. Coming off a broken leg. Worth a 4th if healthy, but the contract structure prevents a move.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed to 2-year, $24M deal with $20M guaranteed. Dead cap would be enormous. Browns need him at center after losing Pocic.
players.injury_notes: Fractured left fibula and ligament damage in ankle during Week 9 of 2025. Released by Packers with failed physical designation. Recovery timeline for 2026 unclear but expected to be ready.

players.name: Teven Jenkins
players.performance_evidence: Re-signed with Browns after playing all 17 games in 2025 with 4 starts at RG. Previously started 43 games for the Bears. Solid run blocker with some pass protection concerns. Provides depth and starting-caliber play at guard.

trade_value.reasoning: Veteran guard on a cheap one-year deal. Adequate starter but nothing special. Worth a late-round pick. More valuable as a depth piece than a trade chip.

players.availability_reasoning: One-year deal, veteran depth piece. If the Browns draft OL or Bitonio returns, Jenkins could be expendable.

players.name: Tytus Howard
players.performance_evidence: Former 1st-round pick with 93 career starts across RT, RG, and LG in Houston. Extremely versatile — started at three OL positions in 2025. PFF grade of 62.8 in 2025 (56th of 84 tackles). Below-average in both PBWR (24th percentile at tackle) and RBWR (31st percentile). Allowed zero sacks last season but overall grades suggest a below-average starter. ESPN's Seth Walder called him a 'below-average starter' who 'isn't cheap.'

trade_value.reasoning: Veteran starting tackle on a new 3-year extension with $43M guaranteed — the dead cap is astronomical, making him essentially untradeable. He's a below-average starter at a premium position, but the contract locks him in.

players.availability_reasoning: Just traded for and signed to a 3-year, $63M extension with $43M guaranteed. Dead cap is $43M. He's the starting RT for the foreseeable future whether they like it or not.

players.name: Alex Wright
players.performance_evidence: Breakout 2025 season opposite Myles Garrett: 5 sacks (career total now 12), 9 TFLs (tied 8th in NFL), 24 tackles, 1 forced fumble through the full season. Strong run defender who benefits from Garrett drawing double teams. Signed 3-year, $33M extension ($21M guaranteed) midseason in November 2025, signaling the organization's commitment.

trade_value.reasoning: 26-year-old starting EDGE on a team-friendly deal ($11M AAV) with 3 years of control. Solid complementary pass rusher who produced well opposite Garrett. EDGE premium applies. Worth a late 2nd-round pick, but dead cap of ~$10M and organizational commitment make a trade unlikely.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a midseason extension. Browns view him as Garrett's long-term running mate. Would take a significant overpay to pry him loose.
players.injury_notes: Torn triceps in 2024 limited him to 4 games. Quad injury sidelined him in Week 10 of 2025. Some durability concerns.

players.name: Maliek Collins
players.performance_evidence: Signed as a free agent in March 2025 from SF. Had a strong season: approximately 5 sacks, 41 tackles. Ranked 3rd on the team in sacks behind Garrett and Wright. Veteran presence who helped develop rookie Mason Graham. 10-year veteran with 30+ career sacks.

trade_value.reasoning: Solid veteran DT on a cheap cap hit ($5.5M) in the final year of his deal. Expiring contract limits value. Dead cap exceeds cap hit due to void-year structure, which complicates a trade. Worth a 5th-round pick to a contender needing DL depth.

players.availability_reasoning: Final year of his deal, veteran on a rebuilding team. If the Browns want to prioritize young DTs (Graham, Mike Hall Jr.), Collins could be moved for draft capital. Dead cap structure is a complication though.

players.name: Mason Graham
players.performance_evidence: 5th overall pick. Started all 17 games as a rookie. 49 tackles, 7 TFL, 0.5 sacks, 4 passes defensed. Named to PFWA All-Rookie team. PFF overall grade of 68.7 (31st of 134 DIs). Ranked 1st among rookie DTs in ESPN's pass rush win rate. Quiet box-score stats but his presence freed up Myles Garrett for a record-setting season. Run stop percentage ranked 4th among rookies. Ascending player with enormous ceiling.

trade_value.reasoning: 22-year-old former top-5 pick on a 4-year rookie deal, named All-Rookie team, led rookies in PRWR. The combination of age, draft pedigree, 4 years of control, and ascending play makes him one of the most valuable non-QB trade assets in the NFL. Essentially untouchable.

players.availability_reasoning: 5th overall pick, 22 years old, 4 years of cheap control, All-Rookie team, cornerstone of the defensive rebuild alongside Garrett. Dead cap of ~$28M makes a trade financially impossible anyway.

players.name: Myles Garrett
players.performance_evidence: 2x DPOY (2023, 2025). Set the NFL single-season sack record with 23 sacks in 2025. Also led the league with 33 TFLs, 39 QB hits, and 60 tackles. Double-teamed or chip-blocked on 186 pass rushes (most since 2018) yet still led league in pressures and sacks when facing extra help. Named 1st-team All-Pro 5 times. 7x Pro Bowler. 125.5 career sacks. The most dominant defensive player in the NFL.

trade_value.reasoning: Reigning DPOY with a record-breaking 23-sack season. However, his 4-year, $160M extension includes a full no-trade clause and over $100M in dead cap if traded. He is literally untradeable. If he COULD be traded, he'd command multiple first-round picks, but the contract structure ensures he stays in Cleveland.

players.availability_reasoning: No-trade clause in his contract. Over $100M in dead cap if traded. Just signed a 4-year, $160M extension in March 2025 after initially requesting a trade. The Browns refused to trade him, made him the highest-paid non-QB in NFL history, and he rescinded the request. He's in Cleveland through 2030.
players.character_notes: Requested a trade in early 2025, publicly stating the Browns weren't in a position to compete. Rescinded the request after signing his $160M extension. Has a no-trade clause. Also notable: 9th speeding ticket since 2017.
players.trade_buzz: The trade saga is over. Garrett signed his extension and committed to Cleveland. No-trade clause makes this a non-issue.

players.name: Mohamoud Diabate
players.performance_evidence: UDFA who has worked his way into a meaningful role. Played special teams and rotational LB snaps in 2025. Limited starter-quality production but showed enough to stick on the roster. With Devin Bush departing and Quincy Williams arriving, Diabate will compete for snaps.

trade_value.reasoning: UDFA on a cheap deal with limited starting experience. Essentially no trade value beyond a conditional late-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth piece. Browns signed Quincy Williams and have Carson Schwesinger. Diabate is expendable if someone wants him.

players.name: Quincy Williams
players.performance_evidence: 1st-team All-Pro in 2023 with the Jets. Career totals in 5 years with NYJ: 554 tackles, 58 TFLs, 21 QB hits, 12.5 sacks in 78 games (73 starts). In 2025: 83 tackles, 3.5 sacks, 7 passes defensed in 13 games. Previous ties to new Browns DC Mike Rutenberg. Physical, instinctive linebacker who excels against the run.

trade_value.reasoning: Former All-Pro LB on a 2-year deal at age 29. Still productive but age is starting to work against him. Dead cap ($10M) complicates a trade in year 1. Worth a 3rd-round pick but the Browns just signed him to fill a specific need (replacing Devin Bush), so they're not moving him.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed to replace Devin Bush and reunite with DC Mike Rutenberg. Core piece of the 2026 defense. Would take a clear overpay.
players.injury_notes: Missed 4 games with a shoulder injury in 2025.

players.name: Denzel Ward
players.performance_evidence: 5x Pro Bowler. Has been one of the NFL's best cornerbacks since entering the league. 2025 stats not fully available but earned his 5th Pro Bowl selection, indicating continued high-level play. Consistently strong in man coverage. The Browns' $30.8M cap hit is the 27th-highest in the NFL.

trade_value.reasoning: Still a Pro Bowl-caliber CB at 29, but the contract is a nightmare for trade purposes. $30.8M cap hit with $29.7M dead cap means trading him saves virtually nothing — the trading team would absorb the full cap hit while the Browns eat almost the same in dead money. Functionally untradeable due to the contract structure despite on-field value.

players.availability_reasoning: The contract makes a trade nearly impossible — dead cap ($29.7M) is almost equal to the cap hit ($30.8M), so there's no financial incentive to trade. Ward is still a very good player and the Browns restructured his deal (converting $2.5M roster bonus to signing bonus) rather than trying to move him. He'll play out 2026 and potentially be a post-June 1 cut candidate in 2027.
players.injury_notes: History of concussions throughout career. Has missed games in multiple seasons due to various injuries but played 17 games in some recent seasons.
players.trade_buzz: Was discussed as a potential cap casualty by NFL.com, but Browns restructured his deal instead. No active trade market due to prohibitive dead cap.

players.name: Tyson Campbell
players.performance_evidence: Former 1st-round pick acquired via trade from Jacksonville in October 2025 (for Greg Newsome II). Played 12 games in 2025 between JAX and CLE: 93 tackles total across the season, 6 passes defended. PFF grades were inconsistent (56.6 overall in CLE, 51.1 coverage). Best season was 2022 with 3 INTs. Good in man coverage and run support. Better fit for Browns' scheme than zone-heavy Jacksonville system. Described as a 'lock-down corner without using much safety help' in Cleveland.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old former 1st-round CB on a below-market deal ($15.2M AAV at a time when top CBs command $25-30M). Two years of control. The Browns effectively got him at a discount because Jacksonville already paid the signing bonus ($16M). Worth a 2nd/3rd round pick given age, pedigree, and contract value.

players.availability_reasoning: The Browns traded for him specifically to be their CB2 long-term. Under contract through 2027 at a very team-friendly rate. Would take significant compensation to move him — he's the future across from Ward.
players.injury_notes: Some injury history in Jacksonville but played through the end of 2025 in Cleveland.

players.name: Myles Harden
players.performance_evidence: Emerged as the starting nickel corner in 2025 after Martin Emerson's Achilles injury. 2nd-year player from Michigan who handled the slot role capably. Allowed the safety group (Delpit, Hickman) to roam more freely. Limited sample but Browns clearly trust him in the role.

trade_value.reasoning: Young nickel corner on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control. Showed enough to earn a starting role but limited track record. Worth a 5th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Young, cheap, and starting. Browns have no reason to move him unless they receive good value.

players.name: Grant Delpit
players.performance_evidence: Best season of his career in 2025: 89 tackles (48 solo), 3.0 sacks, 2 forced fumbles, 2 fumble recoveries, 1 INT, 4 PDs over 17 games. Pro Bowl alternate. Named Browns Walter Payton Man of the Year nominee. Has developed into one of the NFL's better safeties with range, tackling ability, and playmaking. 2019 Thorpe Award winner at LSU.

trade_value.reasoning: 27-year-old Pro Bowl-caliber safety on the final year of a $12M/year deal. Expiring contract reduces value (0.6-0.7x multiplier). A contender needing safety help would pay a 3rd-round pick for one year of high-end play. But the Browns may want him for their 2026 defense and could extend him.

players.availability_reasoning: Final year of his deal, entering a contract year at 27. The Browns could extend him or let him play out 2026 and walk. If they want to maximize value rather than risk losing him for nothing, a mid-season trade is possible. But he's the defense's best secondary player and losing him would hurt.
players.injury_notes: Tore Achilles in 2020 training camp, missing entire rookie season. Has been relatively healthy since. Dealt with minor back and groin issues in 2025 but played 17 games.

players.name: Ronnie Hickman
players.performance_evidence: Assigned a 2nd-round restricted free agent tender worth $5.767M for 2026. Started alongside Delpit in 2025 and was praised by teammates for his versatility and ability to support both run and pass defense. Solid contributor on a defense that allowed the 3rd-fewest passing yards per game. Complements Delpit well.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old starting FS on a one-year RFA tender. If a team signs him to an offer sheet, the Browns would receive a 2nd-round pick as compensation (2nd-round tender). His standalone trade value is around a 4th-round pick given his production level and one year of control.

players.availability_reasoning: On a 2nd-round RFA tender. If another team signs him to an offer sheet, Browns can match or take the 2nd-round pick. Could also be traded for a mid-round pick if the Browns find a replacement in the draft.
players.trade_buzz: Was an RFA this offseason. Browns tendered him at the 2nd-round level, indicating they want him back but aren't committed long-term.

players.name: Jack Stoll
players.performance_evidence: Primarily an inline blocking tight end who spent his first four NFL seasons with the Eagles. Limited receiving production — career-best was 2022 with 16 catches for 120 yards and 1 TD. Valued for his run-blocking ability and special teams work. Not a featured pass-catching option. Signed with Cleveland as a TE2/blocking specialist to complement David Njoku. Likely 10-20 catches per season at most.

trade_value.reasoning: Blocking-specialist TE2 on a modest deal has minimal trade value. Worth a 7th-round pick at most; teams can find comparable blocking tight ends as UDFAs or late-round picks.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth tight end on a cheap deal. Cleveland would move him for any reasonable draft compensation, but there's little demand for a player at this role. Not being shopped, but no reason to hold firm if a late-round pick were offered.

players.name: Jeremiah Owusu-Koramoah
players.performance_evidence: Breakout 2023 season: 101 tackles, 4 sacks, 2 INTs, earned Second-Team All-Pro and Pro Bowl nod. One of the most athletic and versatile linebackers in the NFL — excels in coverage, can rush the passer, and plays sideline-to-sideline. His 2024 season was cut short by a significant neck injury suffered in November 2024 (stinger/cervical spine) which placed serious doubt on his career status before he was eventually cleared. Signed a 4-year extension with Cleveland in 2024 off his All-Pro campaign. When healthy, he's a top-10 off-ball linebacker with rare coverage skills.

trade_value.reasoning: Pro Bowl linebacker entering his prime at 26 with 3 years of control would normally command a high 2nd-round pick equivalent. However, the serious neck injury from 2024 introduces meaningful medical risk that any acquiring team's doctors would scrutinize heavily, and the $22M dead cap makes Cleveland very unlikely to move him. When healthy, a top-10 off-ball LB on a fair deal.

players.availability_reasoning: Cleveland just signed JOK to a long-term extension in 2024, reflecting his importance as the defensive centerpiece alongside Myles Garrett. The massive dead cap ($22M) makes a trade financially devastating. He's the emotional leader of the defense and only 26. Even with the neck scare, Cleveland has committed fully to him. The only scenario where he'd be moved is if medical re-evaluation reveals long-term concerns, and even then the dead cap would likely force them to keep him.
players.injury_notes: Suffered a serious neck/cervical spine injury in November 2024 that ended his season and initially raised career concerns. Was eventually cleared to return. History of minor soft-tissue injuries early in career. The neck injury is the primary medical red flag — any trade partner would require extensive medical review.
